hypothesis: `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual` was the first recent matched probe to land relatively close to the `lowrank_two_stage_residual` K128 anchor, so the next most informative architecture-only question is whether that coarse static codebook branch remains viable when the sparse residual selector is made cheaper. `lowrank_grouped_soft_codebook_residual` preserves the same LUTurbo-compatible static-library decomposition as the soft-codebook probe, but replaces full global residual selection with group-local winner selection. If it stays reasonably close under the exact matched `K=128`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12` recipe, then this family may offer a better quality-versus-online-selection-cost tradeoff than the mainline; if it falls back to the weaker grouped-single-stage pattern, then the earlier soft-codebook result should be treated as requiring full global selection to matter.
summary: Run one matched architecture probe by switching `ARCHITECTURE` to `lowrank_grouped_soft_codebook_residual` while keeping `K=128`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, and `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12` fixed.
self_review: This obeys the round policy by changing exactly one primary variable, `architecture`, while keeping the mainline `K=128`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, and `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12` recipe fixed. It is a stronger LUTurbo-aligned probe than opening a heavier two-stage soft-codebook branch, because grouped local competition directly targets lower online selection cost while retaining the promising coarse static codebook decomposition seen in Round 92. The risk is that the grouped selector may erase most of the quality gain that made `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual` interesting, in which case this family should be deprioritized quickly.
notes_to_memory: Round 93 spends the single allowed matched architecture probe on `lowrank_grouped_soft_codebook_residual` against the exact `lowrank_two_stage_residual` K128/EF12/signum/LR=2.0e-3 anchor.

Judge this run strictly as a family-worthiness test for combining a coarse static codebook branch with cheaper group-local sparse selection; the key question is whether the Round 92 soft-codebook signal survives a more deployment-friendly selector.
Return to the `lowrank_two_stage_residual` mainline next round regardless of outcome; do not turn this into a multi-round soft-codebook branch sequence.
next_hypotheses: If `lowrank_grouped_soft_codebook_residual` is clearly weaker than both `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual` and the matched `lowrank_two_stage_residual` anchor, discard grouped soft-codebook variants and resume mainline tuning next round.

If `lowrank_grouped_soft_codebook_residual` remains surprisingly close to the mainline, record it as a potentially lower-selection-cost branch but still return to `lowrank_two_stage_residual` next round per probe policy.
